title: Ophryosporus foliolosus
intro: Ophryosporus foliolosus is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, also known as "Wild Borage" or "Mexican Borage", which is a type of herbaceous perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ophryosporus foliolosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
